old_claim.note: Instrument Calibration Standards provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: Later chronometer rating and calibration practice must not be projected backward as a construction prerequisite for the 1662 sea-clock prototypes.

source_shape.source_locator: National Museums Scotland documents a purpose-made 1662 pendulum sea clock, its makers, sea trials, and failure as a longitude solution.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The artifact history supports mechanical clockwork as the prototype substrate while treating trial records and later rating practice as context.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The corrected scope leaves this relationship outside the causal boundary. Later chronometer rating and calibration practice must not be projected backward as a construction prerequisite for the 1662 sea-clock prototypes.

ontology_before: marine_chronometer_prototypes treated instrument_calibration_standards as a direct enabling relationship.
ontology_after: Later chronometer rating and calibration practice must not be projected backward as a construction prerequisite for the 1662 sea-clock prototypes.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: marine_chronometer_prototypes no longer has instrument_calibration_standards as a direct prerequisite.
would_reject_if: A direct historical or technical source establishes instrument_calibration_standards as a necessary causal input to the newly scoped marine_chronometer_prototypes claim.
short_reason: Later chronometer rating and calibration practice must not be projected backward as a construction prerequisite for the 1662 sea-clock prototypes.
